text.title: Methodology Contribution Flow (Open Authorship) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Methodology Contribution Flow (Open Authorship) application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every methodology PR uses the template: topic, tier rationale, prior-art search, draft AGENTS.md + 3 content files, evidence sources, attribution preference.
rule.rationale: Template removes drafting friction + forces evidence + tier rationale.

rule.statement: Three review axes: (a) v2 shape compliance, (b) ≥3 sources cited per major claim, (c) no duplication with existing entries. Each reviewed by a different maintainer.
rule.rationale: Three-axis × three-maintainer review prevents single-reviewer bias + missed duplication.

rule.statement: Author retains attribution: name + URL in methodology's maintainers list + CHANGELOG. Contribution under CLA grants faion redistribution rights.
rule.rationale: Visible attribution motivates contribution + builds contributor brand; CLA enables redistribution.

rule.statement: ≥5 accepted contributions trigger revenue-share enrollment (10% of CLI-paid-tier-attributed revenue for cited methodologies). Documented + audited.
rule.rationale: Compensation aligns long-term contribution with revenue; documented policy avoids ad-hoc favoritism.

rule.statement: Contributor methodologies enter standard 12-month review cycle. Contributor invited to refresh; if declined, internal maintainer takes over.
rule.rationale: Same decay rule as internal entries; ensures consistency + prevents zombie content.
